title: Addoners Optimizers
description: High-performance Minecraft optimization mod that dynamically improves FPS and system efficiency while maintaining visual quality.
body:
# Addoners Optimizer

Addoners Optimizer is a client-side performance mod for Minecraft 1.26.1.2 (Fabric) that dynamically adjusts selected video settings based on real-time FPS measurements to help maintain smoother and more stable gameplay performance.

## How it works

The mod samples the player's FPS every second using a rolling average and applies one of three optimization levels:

* **LOW (≥80 FPS)** — minimal adjustments, prioritizing visual quality
* **MEDIUM (50–79 FPS)** — moderate reductions to selected graphics settings
* **AGGRESSIVE (<50 FPS)** — stronger reductions intended to improve frame stability on lower performance systems

A 3-second cooldown between changes helps prevent rapid setting fluctuations.

## Features

* Automatically adjusts:

  * Render distance
  * Particle settings
  * Smooth lighting
  * Shader quality settings
* Optional status HUD displaying:

  * Current FPS
  * Active optimization level
  * Shader detection state
* Shader-aware mode:

  * Reduces optimization aggressiveness when Iris or Canvas shaders are detected
* `.addoners` profile system:

  * JSON-based profiles for hardware tiers or shader packs
  * User-customizable optimization rules
* Built-in profiles for:

  * Quartzglow Shaderpack
  * Skygleam Shaderpack
* In-game configuration support through Mod Menu
* Structured debug logging to `logs/latest.log`

## Adjustable settings

Depending on the active optimization profile, the mod can dynamically modify:

* Render distance (2–32 chunks)
* Particle settings
* Smooth lighting
* Shadow quality levels
* Shader toggles through profile rules

## Requirements

* Minecraft 1.26.1.2
* Fabric Loader 0.18.6 or newer
* Fabric API
* Java 25+
* Mod Menu (optional)

## Configuration

Config location:

`%appdata%/.minecraft/config/teamaddoners/modconfig.json`

Profiles folder:

`%appdata%/.minecraft/config/teamaddoners/profiles/`

No manual setup is required. The optimizer starts automatically when Minecraft launches.

## Performance notes

Performance impact and effectiveness may vary depending on:

* Hardware specifications
* Installed mods
* Shader packs
* World complexity
* Render resolution
* Server load

Testing was primarily performed on systems using Fabric with Sodium-based rendering environments under varying render distances and shader configurations.
